info.title: Orchestrator API
info.description: AI butler orchestrator — real-time agent management, department dispatch, and observability

tags.name: Agents
tags.description: Live agent status, traces, and event streams

tags.name: Departments
tags.description: Six-department system (engineering, quality, operations, protocol, security, personnel)

tags.name: Events
tags.description: Activity events, heatmaps, and category statistics

tags.name: Pipeline
tags.description: Pipeline status, logs, tasks, departments, and scheduler

get.description: Returns the current health status of the orchestrator and its database connection.

uptime.description: Server uptime in seconds, present when status is ok

get.description: Returns a compact overview of open debts, task counts, last collector run, and last analysis timestamp.
Returns up to 50 tasks, ordered by creation date descending. Supports filtering by status and department.

parameters.name: department
parameters.description: Filter by department name (matched against spec.department)

post.description: Creates a task with source 'manual' and returns the new task ID.

reason.description: Reason or context for the task

spec.description: Arbitrary specification object (may include department, project, cwd, cognitive_mode, blast_radius)

get.summary: Get a single task by ID
get.description: Returns the full task object including parsed spec.

404.description: Task not found or DB unavailable

post.description: Transitions a task from awaiting_approval to running via the Governor CLI. Broadcasts a task_update WebSocket event on success.

400.description: Invalid task ID or approval error

get.summary: List attention debts with optional filters
get.description: Returns up to 50 attention debts, ordered by severity (high first) then creation date descending.
Returns all currently running agents with their task info, current activity, elapsed time, and recent events.

get.summary: Get full execution trace for a task
get.description: Returns the task details, a trace summary (turns, tools, errors, decisions, cost), and all agent events.

get.summary: Get agent events for a specific task
get.description: Returns agent events for the given task ID, ordered by ID ascending.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of events to return (max 500)

get.description: Server-Sent Events stream that emits real-time agent events across all running tasks. Sends an initial backlog of the last 20 events, then polls every 1 second for new events.

parameters.name: since_id
parameters.description: Only return events with ID greater than this value

schema.description: SSE stream. Each message is JSON with either {type: 'backlog', events: AgentEvent[]} or {type: 'agent_event', event: AgentEvent}

get.description: Returns an array of department key strings.
Returns the department's SKILL.md content, guidelines, recent run log entries, learned skills, and suggestions.
Returns the raw markdown content of the department's SKILL.md file.
Returns recent run log entries for the department, ordered most recent first.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of run entries to return (max 100)

get.description: Returns all guideline markdown files for the department.
Returns a map of scenario names to their descriptions and involved departments.

full_audit.description: Full system audit: security + quality + protocol in parallel

post.description: Runs a named scenario via the Governor, dispatching tasks to all departments in the scenario in parallel.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Scenario name (e.g. full_audit, code_and_review, system_health, deep_scan, full_pipeline)

get.summary: Get daily summaries and user profile
get.description: Returns the last 7 daily summaries and the latest user profile.

items.description: Daily summary row from the database

get.description: Returns the most recently generated insights data.

schema.description: Parsed insights JSON (structure depends on the analysis pipeline)

get.description: Returns the last 7 daily summaries with parsed summary content.

summary.description: Present when summary is plain text

items.description: Summary object — if the stored summary is JSON, its fields are spread into the object

get.description: Returns the most recent profile analysis data.

schema.description: Parsed profile analysis JSON (may include commentary, daily_note, and other fields)

get.description: Returns the last 30 profile analysis entries with commentary, daily notes, and voice information.

voice.description: Voice reference ID used on that date

post.description: Accepts the request immediately (202) and runs the profile analyst CLI in the background. Broadcasts profile_analysis_done or profile_analysis_error via WebSocket when complete.

get.description: Returns up to 200 events from the specified time window, ordered by occurrence date descending.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Number of days to look back

get.summary: Get event count heatmap by day
get.description: Returns daily event counts for building a heatmap visualization.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Number of days to look back

get.description: Returns aggregated event counts and total duration grouped by source.
Returns aggregated event duration and count grouped by category within the specified time window.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Number of days to look back

get.description: Returns experience entries ordered by date descending. Supports filtering by type.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of entries to return (max 100)

get.description: Returns a comprehensive status of all pipeline components: collectors, analysis, governance, departments, and scheduler.

scheduler.description: Key-value pairs from the scheduler_status table

get.description: Returns the most recent log entries from the logs table.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of log entries to return (max 200)

get.summary: Get recent pipeline tasks with parsed metadata
get.description: Returns tasks with flattened spec fields (department, cognitive_mode, blast_radius) for pipeline overview.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of tasks to return (max 100)

get.summary: Get department run statistics from file system
get.description: Returns per-department statistics derived from run-log.jsonl files: total runs, recent runs, success rate, and suggestion availability.

success_rate.description: Ratio of done runs to total runs (0.0 to 1.0)

get.description: Returns next scheduled times for collectors and analysis, and whether a task is currently running.

post.description: Accepts text and returns immediately. The actual audio is broadcast via WebSocket as a 'soul_voice' event with base64-encoded MP3 data. Supports sentence-level splitting with silence gaps.

reference_id.description: Voice reference ID override. If omitted, uses the daily voice.

get.description: Proxies a health check to the upstream TTS service.

status.description: Health status from TTS service, or 'unavailable' if unreachable

get.description: Server-Sent Events stream that emits log entries in real time. Sends an initial backlog of the last 50 logs, then polls every 1 second for new entries.

schema.description: SSE stream. Each message is JSON with either {type: 'backlog', logs: LogEntry[]} or {type: 'append', logs: LogEntry[]}

spec.description: Task specification (parsed JSON). May include department, project, cwd, cognitive_mode, blast_radius, etc.

data.description: Parsed event payload. For agent_turn: may include turn, tools, thinking, text, error. For agent_result: may include stop_reason, is_error, duration_ms, cost_usd.
